sn11.11:1.1:
1
At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he adopted and undertook seven vows by the undertaking of which he achieved the status of Sakka.
What were the seven vows?
sn11.11:2.2: (1) "‘As long as I live may I support my parents.’
sn11.11:2.3: (2) "‘As long as I live may I respect the family elders.’
sn11.11:2.4: (3) "‘As long as I live may I speak gently.’
sn11.11:2.5: (4) "‘As long as I live may I not speak divisively.’
sn11.11:2.6:
(5) "‘As long as I live may I dwell at home with a mind devoid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, delighting in relinquishment, devoted to charity,
delighting in giving and sharing.’
sn11.11:2.7:
(6) "‘As long as I live may I speak the truth.’
(7) "‘As long as I live may I be free from anger, and if anger should arise in me may I dispel it quickly.’
sn11.11:2.8: "In the past, bhikkhus, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he adopted and undertook these seven vows by the undertaking of which he achieved the status of Sakka.
sn11.11:3.2:
2
"When a person supports his parents,
And respects the family elders;
When his speech is gentle and courteous,
And he refrains from divisive words;
3
When he strives to remove meanness,
Is truthful, and vanquishes anger,
The Tāvatiṁsa devas call him
Truly a superior person."
